resume_snapshot.next_step: This message appears to be a built-in Claude Code system message, not from your custom hooks. The text "TERMINAL_ID environment variable not set. Multi-terminal isolation requires unique terminal ID." isn't in any of you

decision_register.summary: Acknowledged. The stop hook is reminding me that multi-terminal isolation requires `TERMINAL_ID` to be set. This doesn't change the GTO situation - the skill still can't run because the monorepo packages don't have individual `.git` directories. If you want to run GTO, you'd need to either: 1. Run it from within the git repo root (`P:\`) 2. Point it at a package that's a git submodule Otherwise, the `/critique on /s` we completed earlier remains the authoritative gap analysis for the `/s` skill.
